methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The actual ERC20 token balance of the pool must always be greater than or equal to the tracked available liquidity plus the total bid amount.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract's actual balance is less than the tracked liquidity, users will not be able to withdraw their funds or liquidators won't get their bids back, leading to a bank run and insolvency.

invariants.description: The total supplied assets (including accrued fees) must equal the available liquidity plus total borrowed assets across all groups.
invariants.attack_scenario: If debt grows faster than supply, or liquidity is drained without updating supply, the protocol becomes undercollateralized internally, stealing yield from depositors.
invariants.invariant_logic: (assetData.totalScaledCrossSupply + assetData.accruedFee) * assetData.supplyIndex ≈ assetData.availableLiquidity + sum(group.totalScaledCrossBorrow * group.borrowIndex) + sum(group.totalScaledIsolateBorrow * group.borrowIndex)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_accounting_conservation(address asset) public view { uint256 totalSupply = (assetData.totalScaledCrossSupply + assetData.accruedFee) * assetData.supplyIndex; uint256 totalDebt = getTotalBorrow(assetData); assert(totalSupply >= assetData.availableLiquidity + totalDebt - TOLERANCE && totalSupply <= assetData.availableLiquidity + totalDebt + TOLERANCE); }

invariants.description: A user's Health Factor must remain above the liquidation threshold after any borrow, withdrawal, or supply mode change.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker manipulates their position to become undercollateralized without being liquidated, leaving bad debt in the protocol.

invariants.description: The supply and borrow indices must be monotonically increasing.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an index decreases, it would retroactively erase user deposits or reduce borrower debt, leading to yield theft or unbacked debt.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_index_monotonicity() public { uint256 oldSupplyIndex = assetData.supplyIndex; updateInterestIndexs(); assert(assetData.supplyIndex >= oldSupplyIndex); }

invariants.description: In an isolated NFT auction, each new bid must be strictly greater than the previous bid by at least the minimum bid delta.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker could place a bid equal to or lower than the current highest bid, griefing the auction process and preventing the protocol from recovering bad debt.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_auction_bid_monotonicity(uint256 newBid, uint256 oldBid, uint256 borrowAmount) public { assert(newBid >= oldBid + (borrowAmount * 0.01)); }

invariants.description: The pool's available liquidity must not decrease after a flash loan execution.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker uses a reentrancy or callback exploit during a flash loan to drain tokens without repaying, bypassing the repayment check.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_flashloan_conservation() public { uint256 preBalance = IERC20(asset).balanceOf(address(this)); executeFlashLoanERC20(); assert(IERC20(asset).balanceOf(address(this)) >= preBalance); }

invariants.description: The value of the collateral liquidated must not exceed the value of the debt repaid plus the maximum liquidation bonus.
invariants.attack_scenario: A liquidator exploits a rounding error or oracle manipulation to seize more collateral than permitted, draining value from the borrower unfairly.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_liquidation_profitability() public { assert(actualCollateralToLiquidate * collateralPrice <= actualDebtToLiquidate * debtPrice * (1 + liquidationBonus)); }

invariants.description: If an isolated loan is active or in auction, the underlying NFT collateral must be locked by the protocol.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker manages to withdraw or transfer an NFT that is actively being used as collateral for an isolated loan, leaving the loan unbacked.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_nft_locked_when_borrowed(uint256 tokenId) public view { if (loanLookup[tokenId].loanStatus != 0) { assert(erc721TokenData[tokenId].lockerAddr != address(0)); } }
